111	// requireLoopback enforces that addr's host is a loopback address. An IP literal
112	// must satisfy IsLoopback; a hostname (e.g. "localhost") is accepted only if it
113	// resolves to at least one address and EVERY resolved address is loopback — a
114	// hostname that maps to any routable address is refused. An empty host (":9095")
115	// binds every interface and is refused.
116	func requireLoopback(addr string) error {
117		host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr)
118		if err != nil {
119			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: invalid listen address %q: %w", addr, err)
120		}
121		if host == "" {
122			return fmt.Errorf("%w: empty host in %q binds all interfaces", ErrNonLoopbackBind, addr)
123		}
124	
125		if ip, err := netip.ParseAddr(host); err == nil {
126			if !ip.IsLoopback() {
127				return fmt.Errorf("%w: got %q", ErrNonLoopbackBind, addr)
128			}
129			return nil
130		}
131	
132		// Hostname: resolve and require every resolved address to be loopback.
133		ips, err := net.LookupIP(host)
134		if err != nil {
135			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: cannot resolve listen host %q: %w", host, err)
136		}
137		if len(ips) == 0 {
138			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: listen host %q resolved to no addresses", host)
139		}
140		for _, ip := range ips {
141			if !ip.IsLoopback() {
142				return fmt.Errorf("%w: host %q resolves to non-loopback %s", ErrNonLoopbackBind, host, ip)
143			}
144		}
145		return nil
146	}
147	
148	// verifyLoopbackBind is the fail-closed, act-then-verify half of the loopback
149	// guard: it asserts that the address the kernel ACTUALLY bound (ln.Addr()) is a
150	// loopback TCP address, independent of any DNS resolution. Because net.Listen
151	// resolves a hostname listen address itself — a second, independent lookup from
152	// requireLoopback's — verifying the concrete bound address is the only check
153	// that a resolver cannot subvert. A non-TCP or non-loopback bound address
154	// yields ErrNonLoopbackBind so the caller can close the listener and refuse.
155	func verifyLoopbackBind(a net.Addr) error {
156		tcp, ok := a.(*net.TCPAddr)
157		if !ok {
158			return fmt.Errorf("%w: bound to non-TCP address %v", ErrNonLoopbackBind, a)
159		}
160		if !tcp.IP.IsLoopback() {
161			return fmt.Errorf("%w: bound to non-loopback %s", ErrNonLoopbackBind, tcp.IP)
162		}
163		return nil
164	}

55		ln, err := net.Listen("tcp", addr)
56		if err != nil {
57			return nil, fmt.Errorf("metrics: listen on %q: %w", addr, err)
58		}
59		// Act-then-verify: net.Listen performs its OWN address resolution,
60		// independent of the requireLoopback pre-check above. For a hostname listen
61		// address a resolver whose answers differ between the two lookups (TOCTOU)
62		// could get the endpoint bound to a routable interface even though the
63		// pre-check passed. Assert the address the kernel actually bound is
64		// loopback and fail closed otherwise — this removes any dependence on
65		// resolver trust.
66		if err := verifyLoopbackBind(ln.Addr()); err != nil {
67			_ = ln.Close()
68			return nil, err
69		}
70	
71		mux := http.NewServeMux()
72		mux.Handle(metricsPath, promhttp.HandlerFor(reg, promhttp.HandlerOpts{
73			ErrorHandling: promhttp.HTTPErrorOnError,
74		}))
75	
76		return &Server{
77			ln: ln,
78			srv: &http.Server{
79				Handler:           mux,
80				ReadHeaderTimeout: readHeaderTimeout,
81			},
82			log: logger.Component("metrics"),
83		}, nil
84	}
85	
86	// Addr returns the actual bound listen address (with the resolved port).
87	func (s *Server) Addr() net.Addr { return s.ln.Addr() }
88	
89	// URL returns the scrape URL for the endpoint, suitable for the Fetch helper.
90	func (s *Server) URL() string {
91		return fmt.Sprintf("http://%s%s", s.ln.Addr().String(), metricsPath)
92	}
93	
94	// Start serves the endpoint in a background goroutine and returns immediately.
95	// A Serve error other than the expected http.ErrServerClosed (from Close) is
96	// logged; the listener is already bound, so Start itself cannot fail.
97	func (s *Server) Start() {
98		s.log.Info("metrics endpoint listening", "addr", s.ln.Addr().String(), "path", metricsPath)
99		go func() {
100			if err := s.srv.Serve(s.ln); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, http.ErrServerClosed) {
101				s.log.Error("metrics endpoint serve error", "err", err.Error())
102			}
103		}()
104	}
105	
106	// Close gracefully shuts the endpoint down, bounded by ctx.
107	func (s *Server) Close(ctx context.Context) error {
108		return s.srv.Shutdown(ctx)
109	}
110	
111	// requireLoopback enforces that addr's host is a loopback address. An IP literal
112	// must satisfy IsLoopback; a hostname (e.g. "localhost") is accepted only if it
113	// resolves to at least one address and EVERY resolved address is loopback — a
114	// hostname that maps to any routable address is refused. An empty host (":9095")
115	// binds every interface and is refused.
116	func requireLoopback(addr string) error {
117		host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(addr)
118		if err != nil {
119			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: invalid listen address %q: %w", addr, err)
120		}
121		if host == "" {
122			return fmt.Errorf("%w: empty host in %q binds all interfaces", ErrNonLoopbackBind, addr)
123		}
124	
125		if ip, err := netip.ParseAddr(host); err == nil {
126			if !ip.IsLoopback() {
127				return fmt.Errorf("%w: got %q", ErrNonLoopbackBind, addr)
128			}
129			return nil
130		}
131	
132		// Hostname: resolve and require every resolved address to be loopback.
133		ips, err := net.LookupIP(host)
134		if err != nil {
135			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: cannot resolve listen host %q: %w", host, err)
136		}
137		if len(ips) == 0 {
138			return fmt.Errorf("metrics: listen host %q resolved to no addresses", host)
139		}
140		for _, ip := range ips {
141			if !ip.IsLoopback() {
142				return fmt.Errorf("%w: host %q resolves to non-loopback %s", ErrNonLoopbackBind, host, ip)
143			}
144		}
145		return nil
146	}
147	
148	// verifyLoopbackBind is the fail-closed, act-then-verify half of the loopback
149	// guard: it asserts that the address the kernel ACTUALLY bound (ln.Addr()) is a
150	// loopback TCP address, independent of any DNS resolution. Because net.Listen
151	// resolves a hostname listen address itself — a second, independent lookup from
152	// requireLoopback's — verifying the concrete bound address is the only check
153	// that a resolver cannot subvert. A non-TCP or non-loopback bound address
154	// yields ErrNonLoopbackBind so the caller can close the listener and refuse.
155	func verifyLoopbackBind(a net.Addr) error {
156		tcp, ok := a.(*net.TCPAddr)
157		if !ok {
158			return fmt.Errorf("%w: bound to non-TCP address %v", ErrNonLoopbackBind, a)
159		}
160		if !tcp.IP.IsLoopback() {
161			return fmt.Errorf("%w: bound to non-loopback %s", ErrNonLoopbackBind, tcp.IP)
162		}
163		return nil
164	}
```

64		// loopback and fail closed otherwise — this removes any dependence on
65		// resolver trust.
66		if err := verifyLoopbackBind(ln.Addr()); err != nil {

11	// requiredMode is the exact permission bits the config file must carry. The file
12	// holds the WireGuard private key and the outer-control PSK, so it must not be
13	// readable by group or others.
14	const requiredMode fs.FileMode = 0o600
15	
16	// Load reads, validates, and returns the configuration at path. It fails fast:
17	// the file must have mode 0600 exactly, must be valid TOML, and must satisfy the
18	// required-field invariants.
19	func Load(path string) (*Config, error) {
20		info, err := os.Stat(path)
21		if err != nil {
22			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
23		}
24		if perm := info.Mode().Perm(); perm != requiredMode {
25			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config %s: insecure permissions %#o, must be %#o (holds private key and PSK)", path, perm, requiredMode)
26		}
27	
28		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
29		if err != nil {
30			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
31		}
32	
33		var c Config
34		if err := toml.Unmarshal(data, &c); err != nil {
35			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config %s: %w", path, err)
36		}
37		if err := c.normalize(); err != nil {
38			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config %s: %w", path, err)
39		}
40		if err := c.validate(); err != nil {
41			return nil, fmt.Errorf("config %s: %w", path, err)
42		}
43		return &c, nil
44	}
```

481	// entry must parse as netip.AddrPort ("host:port"), the same format
482	// bind.Multipath.ParseEndpoint requires of the UAPI endpoint string at
483	// runtime, and duplicates within the list are rejected.
484	func (p *Peer) resolveEndpoints() error {
485		if p.Endpoint != "" && len(p.EndpointsRaw) > 0 {
486			return errors.New("endpoint and endpoints are mutually exclusive; endpoint is the single-entry legacy form of endpoints")
487		}
488		raw := p.EndpointsRaw
489		if p.Endpoint != "" {
490			raw = []string{p.Endpoint}
491		}
492		seen := make(map[netip.AddrPort]struct{}, len(raw))
493		endpoints := make([]netip.AddrPort, 0, len(raw))
494		for _, s := range raw {
495			ap, err := netip.ParseAddrPort(s)
496			if err != nil {
497				return fmt.Errorf("invalid endpoint %q: %w", s, err)
498			}
499			if _, dup := seen[ap]; dup {
500				return fmt.Errorf("duplicate endpoint %q", s)
501			}
502			seen[ap] = struct{}{}
503			endpoints = append(endpoints, ap)
504		}
505		p.Endpoints = endpoints
506		return nil
507	}

1	package log

10	// Field key conventions. Per-path events carry FieldPath so logs can be filtered

13		FieldComponent = "component"

17	// Logger is the structured-logging surface the rest of wanbond depends on, so no
18	// package imports log/slog directly. It supports component- and path-scoped
19	// child loggers whose scope attributes are attached to every subsequent record.
20	type Logger interface {
21		Debug(msg string, args ...any)
22		Info(msg string, args ...any)
23		Warn(msg string, args ...any)
24		Error(msg string, args ...any)
25		// Component returns a child logger tagged with the given component name.
26		Component(name string) Logger
27		// Path returns a child logger tagged with the given path name.
28		Path(name string) Logger
29	}
30	
31	// slogLogger is the slog-backed implementation of Logger.
32	type slogLogger struct {
33		l *slog.Logger
34	}
35	
36	// New builds a JSON-handler Logger writing to w at the given level. An unknown
37	// level is a configuration error (fail fast); an empty level defaults to info.
38	func New(level string, w io.Writer) (Logger, error) {
39		lvl, err := parseLevel(level)
40		if err != nil {
41			return nil, err
42		}
43		h := slog.NewJSONHandler(w, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: lvl})
44		return &slogLogger{l: slog.New(h)}, nil
45	}
46	
47	func parseLevel(level string) (slog.Level, error) {
48		switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(level)) {
49		case "", "info":
50			return slog.LevelInfo, nil
51		case "debug":
52			return slog.LevelDebug, nil
53		case "warn", "warning":
54			return slog.LevelWarn, nil
55		case "error":
56			return slog.LevelError, nil
57		default:
58			return 0, fmt.Errorf("log: unknown level %q (want debug|info|warn|error)", level)
59		}
60	}
61	
62	func (s *slogLogger) Debug(msg string, args ...any) { s.l.Debug(msg, args...) }
63	func (s *slogLogger) Info(msg string, args ...any)  { s.l.Info(msg, args...) }
64	func (s *slogLogger) Warn(msg string, args ...any)  { s.l.Warn(msg, args...) }
65	func (s *slogLogger) Error(msg string, args ...any) { s.l.Error(msg, args...) }
66	
67	func (s *slogLogger) Component(name string) Logger {
68		return &slogLogger{l: s.l.With(FieldComponent, name)}
69	}
70

486			return errors.New("endpoint and endpoints are mutually exclusive; endpoint is the single-entry legacy form of endpoints")
487		}
488		raw := p.EndpointsRaw
489		if p.Endpoint != "" {
490			raw = []string{p.Endpoint}
491		}
492		seen := make(map[netip.AddrPort]struct{}, len(raw))
493		endpoints := make([]netip.AddrPort, 0, len(raw))
494		for _, s := range raw {
495			ap, err := netip.ParseAddrPort(s)
496			if err != nil {
497				return fmt.Errorf("invalid endpoint %q: %w", s, err)
498			}
499			if _, dup := seen[ap]; dup {
500				return fmt.Errorf("duplicate endpoint %q", s)
501			}
502			seen[ap] = struct{}{}
503			endpoints = append(endpoints, ap)
504		}
505		p.Endpoints = endpoints
506		return nil
507	}
508	
509	// Amnezia holds the amneziawg-go obfuscation parameters. They must match on both
510	// ends for the handshake to succeed; they are defense-in-depth only.

145		// and AddPath do, so DATA and PROBE agree on the wire and the promoted path is never
146		// renumbered.
147		ps := &pathState{name: dp.def.Name, id: dp.prober.PathID(), src: dp.def.SourceAddr, conn: c, codec: codec, prober: dp.prober}
148		switch {
149		case dp.def.DestAddr.IsValid():
150			ps.setRemote(dp.def.DestAddr)
151		case m.hasDefaultRemote:
152			ps.setRemote(m.defaultRemote)
153		}
154		// Append to the path slice, then admit the prober to the scheduler as the new tail;
155		// both are index-aligned, so the scheduler's returned index must equal the new path's
156		// slice index. A mismatch would mis-route datagrams, so fail loudly and roll back.
157		m.paths = append(m.paths, ps)
158		schedIdx, err := dyn.AddPath(ps.prober)
159		if err != nil {
160			m.paths = m.paths[:len(m.paths)-1]
161			return err
162		}
163		if schedIdx != len(m.paths)-1 {
164			bindIdx := len(m.paths) - 1
165			_ = dyn.RemovePath(schedIdx)
166			m.paths = m.paths[:len(m.paths)-1]
167			return fmt.Errorf("bind: scheduler/path index skew after deferred promote: sched=%d bind=%d", schedIdx, bindIdx)
168		}
169		// Spawn the promoted path's Bind-owned reader on the CURRENT Open span's delivery
170		// signal; readersWG tracks it so a subsequent Close joins it (no goroutine leak).
171		m.readersWG.Add(1)
172		go m.readLoop(ps, m.deliverSignal)
173		return nil
174	}
175

96		lastSwitch time.Time

102	// fake health, and fake remote/rehandshake.
103	func newHubFailover(endpoints []netip.AddrPort, health []hubHealth, remote peerRemote, rh rehandshake, clock telemetry.Clock, settle time.Duration, lg log.Logger) *hubFailover {

148		}

161

163		// against the new endpoint, then initiate the fresh WG re-handshake toward it.
164		h.remote.SetPeerRemote(next)
165		if h.rehandshake != nil {
166			h.rehandshake()
167		}
168		h.log.Warn("hub failover: all paths to active concentrator down; switched endpoint and re-handshaked",
169			"from_index", prev, "to_index", h.idx, "to_endpoint", next.String(), "endpoints", len(h.endpoints))
170	}

102	// fake health, and fake remote/rehandshake.
103	func newHubFailover(endpoints []netip.AddrPort, health []hubHealth, remote peerRemote, rh rehandshake, clock telemetry.Clock, settle time.Duration, lg log.Logger) *hubFailover {
104		return &hubFailover{

112			log:         lg,
113		}
114	}

116	// allDownLocked reports HUB LOSS: every path's liveness to the active concentrator is
117	// DOWN simultaneously. An empty health set is NOT hub loss (there is nothing to declare
118	// dead — a bind without the probe transport never drives this). Caller holds mu.
119	func (h *hubFailover) allDownLocked() bool {
120		if len(h.health) == 0 {
121			return false
122		}
123		for _, hp := range h.health {
124			if hp.State() != telemetry.StateDown {
125				return false
126			}
127		}
128		return true
129	}

132	// endpoint's settle dwell has elapsed) it advances to the next endpoint, repoints the
133	// bond's remote, and initiates a fresh re-handshake. It is idempotent and cheap in the
134	// steady state (any path up, or a single-endpoint list) — a length check plus a liveness
135	// sweep — so a periodic loop may call it at the probe cadence.
136	func (h *hubFailover) check() {
137		h.mu.Lock()
138		defer h.mu.Unlock()
139	
140		// GUARD: a single-endpoint (or empty) list takes NO failover action — no advance, no
141		// remote repoint, no re-handshake — so a one-concentrator deployment is byte-for-byte
142		// the pre-T57 behaviour.
143		if len(h.endpoints) < 2 {
144			return
145		}
146		if !h.allDownLocked() {
147			return
148		}

150		// before advancing, so a not-yet-echoed (transiently DOWN) healthy endpoint is not
151		// skipped. Applies at boot (endpoint 0) and after every switch.
152		now := h.clock.Now()
153		if now.Sub(h.lastSwitch) < h.settle {
154			return
155		}
156	
157		prev := h.idx
158		h.idx = (h.idx + 1) % len(h.endpoints)
159		next := h.endpoints[h.idx]
160		h.lastSwitch = now
161	
162		// Repoint the DATA/PROBE plane FIRST so probes immediately start re-arming detection
163		// against the new endpoint, then initiate the fresh WG re-handshake toward it.
164		h.remote.SetPeerRemote(next)
165		if h.rehandshake != nil {
166			h.rehandshake()
167		}
168		h.log.Warn("hub failover: all paths to active concentrator down; switched endpoint and re-handshaked",
169			"from_index", prev, "to_index", h.idx, "to_endpoint", next.String(), "endpoints", len(h.endpoints))
170	}

189			// handoff); ExpireCurrentKeypairs also backdates lastSentHandshake so the immediately
190			// following initiation is not suppressed by the RekeyTimeout guard.
191			peer.ExpireCurrentKeypairs()
192			_ = peer.SendHandshakeInitiation(false)
193		}

215				health[i] = pr
216			}
217			ctrl := newHubFailover(
218				peer.Endpoints, health, mp,
219				deviceRehandshake(dev, peer.PublicKey),
220				telemetry.SystemClock{}, hubFailoverSettle,
221				lg.Component("hubfailover"),
222			)
223			// Poll at the probe cadence: check is cheap (a length check plus a liveness sweep)
224			// and the settle dwell bounds actual switches, so a responsive poll only tightens
225			// detection latency without churning the remote.
226			return ctrl.startHubFailoverLoop(telemetry.DefaultProbeInterval)
227		}

239		if len(h.endpoints) < 2 || interval <= 0 {
240			return func() {}
241		}
242		done := make(chan struct{})
243		go func() {
244			ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
245			defer ticker.Stop()
246			for {
247				select {
248				case <-done:
249					return
250				case <-ticker.C:
251					h.check()
252				}
253			}
254		}()
255		var once sync.Once
256		return func() { once.Do(func() { close(done) }) }

I now have a thoroughly grounded picture of the refactor surface. Here is my candidate plan.

### Session summary
- **Did:** Grounded read-only in G5's state (all 8 clarifying questions Q29-Q36 answered) and the actual refactor surface via codegraph — config endpoint parsing, the multipath remote/resequencer machinery, the hub-failover controller, the T55 reconcile loop, and the wire-audit harness — then emitted a candidate task-DAG.
- **Achieved:** A 14-task / 5-milestone candidate DAG proposing: a new `internal/resolve` resolver subsystem (seam + system + first-class DoH/DoT per Q33), an overloaded-endpoint config surface gated by a per-peer opt-in flag (Q35), a shared mutable locked endpoint set that hub-failover and re-resolution co-own (Q34), multi-record A/AAAA expansion feeding the failover list (Q32), defer-and-reconcile boot + a re-resolution loop with no-op suppression (Q30/Q31), and the netns/unit/wire-audit acceptance bar (Q36).
- **Discovered:** Three answers materially widen blast radius beyond the goal's "recommended" direction: (1) Q34 requires `hubFailover.endpoints` — today explicitly `Immutable after construction. IP:port only` (failover.go:73-76) — to become mutable and co-owned under a shared lock; the existing `hubFailover.mu` already guards `idx`/`lastSwitch`, so it is the natural home. (2) Q32's multi-record expansion means a re-resolve can add/remove standbys, so the active endpoint must be preserved by IDENTITY not index across mutation. (3) `SetPeerRemote` (multipath.go:1371) unconditionally calls `resequencer.Rebaseline()` (D32), so re-resolution MUST suppress unchanged-IP repoints or every poll drops the session. Q33's DoH/DoT choice adds a whole TLS-resolver subsystem (with a bootstrap-IP chicken-and-egg to solve).
- **Issues:** The re-resolution controller must run even for a SINGLE-endpoint hostname config (to track a changing IP), whereas hub-failover only starts at `len(endpoints) >= 2` — so re-resolution is a distinct controller sharing the endpoint set, not a hub-failover extension. This coupling is the highest-risk area and is concentrated in tasks 7/8/10.
